content.boundary: Applies to public funding rules and conditions attached to 6/8-year gimnázium programmes specifically; does not alter admissions criteria, selection ages, or track existence directly, but creates fiscal incentives for maintainers to self-reform.
content.change_lever: Introduce a funding-conditionality mechanism where continued or enhanced per-pupil funding for 6/8-year gimnázium programmes is contingent on demonstrated progress in socioeconomic diversity of admitted cohorts, monitored via existing ABC testing infrastructure.
content.name: Uncouple public funding parity from private-preparation-dependent admission (fiscal-lever reform)
content.system_problem: Selective elite gimnáziums and disadvantaged rural/segregated schools receive identical per-pupil public funding, yet access to elite tracks depends partly on private exam preparation rather than raw ability alone, creating a fiscal-equity mismatch that current funding rules do not address [F-live-education_finance-06].
